---
title: ""
pagetitle: "WISSPAR — Immunogenicity database for pneumococcal vaccines"
page-layout: full
---

::: {.wisspar-hero}
![](assets/images/logo-new.png){.mascot fig-alt="WISSPAR pneumococcus mascot"}

::: {.hero-text}
# WISSPAR

### A curated immunogenicity database

The **W**orldwide **I**ndex of **S**erotype **S**pecific **P**neumococcal
**A**ntibody **R**esponses is a centralized repository for immunogenicity data
from pneumococcal vaccine clinical trials. Information is curated primarily from
the outcome tables on
[clinicaltrials.gov](https://clinicaltrials.gov){target="_blank"} and includes
product details, manufacturers, dosing schedules, age groups, and geographic
regions.
:::
:::

::: {.wisspar-caveat}
**Interpret with caution.** Immunogenicity data should be compared and
interpreted carefully. Data on immunogenicity alone **cannot** be used to infer
differences in effectiveness between vaccines. These measurements need to be
combined with information on the protective concentration of antibodies required
to protect against each serotype in different populations for meaningful
comparisons. Caution should also be used when comparing data from trials
conducted by different sponsors, which may use different assays.
:::

## About the project

WISSPAR is a collaborative partnership between the
[Yale School of Public Health](https://ysph.yale.edu){target="_blank"} and the
[Johns Hopkins Bloomberg School of Public Health](https://publichealth.jhu.edu){target="_blank"},
funded by the [Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation](https://www.gatesfoundation.org){target="_blank"}.
Custom private data-access portals are available upon request — see the
[About page](about.qmd) to get in touch.
